definition: Mathematically grounded techniques for specifying modeling and verifying computational systems.
Reasoning artifacts checked by software against formal rules.
A precise statement of required system behavior.
A condition required before a procedure runs.
A condition required after a procedure completes.
A property that should remain true across execution or state transitions.
A claim that must be proved or discharged for verification.
Checking whether a formal model satisfies a property.
A system that checks machine readable proofs.
A relation showing that concrete implementation preserves abstract specification behavior.
